title: Next.js Pages Router i18n middleware bypass via locale-less /_next/data/<buildId>/<page>.json requests
description: In getResolveRoutes() in packages/next/src/server/lib/router-utils/resolve-routes.ts, when an application uses the Pages Router with i18n configured, an incoming /_next/data/<buildId>/<page>.json request is first normalized by stripping the data-route prefix, then run through normalizeLocalePath against the configured locales. When the request carries no locale prefix (a locale-less data route), curLocaleResult.detectedLocale is empty, so the i18n block only enters the `if (curLocaleResult.detectedLocale)` branch when a locale is present and otherwise leaves the normalized pathname locale-less. Because the middleware/proxy matcher expects the locale-prefixed internal pathname shape that direct page requests are normalized to, the locale-less normalized data pathname fails to match the middleware matcher, so authorization-enforcing middleware never runs for that route. An attacker can therefore retrieve SSR JSON for protected pages by requesting /_next/data/<buildId>/<page>.json without a locale prefix, bypassing the middleware-based authorization checks entirely.
